steps.title: Connect discovery to naming
steps.content: The first sentence says Fusa Miyake identified the carbon-14 spike. The second says the radiocarbon surge was named a Miyake event in honor of its discoverer. The naming is appropriate because it recognizes her discovery.

steps.title: Choose the appropriate signal
steps.content: Choice B would compare similar things, Choice C would contrast the sentences, and Choice D would restate the first sentence. The needed transition shows that the naming was fitting or appropriate. The answer is A.
